text.title: Scope Creep Prevention on Hourly Engagements Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Scope Creep Prevention on Hourly Engagements application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Contract states a weekly budget (e.g., 20 hours/week). When budget exhausted, work pauses until next week OR explicit overage approval.
rule.rationale: Without explicit weekly gate, hourly engagement becomes uncapped; gate is the safety valve.

rule.statement: Document which activities are billable: heads-down work, meetings, written async. Non-billable: brief 'quick question' Slack, ad-hoc check-ins under 5 min.
rule.rationale: Without taxonomy, freelancers either under-bill (resentment) or over-bill (client surprise); explicit list prevents both.

rule.statement: Any new task >2h estimated requires written confirmation BEFORE work starts. Verbal approval doesn't count.
rule.rationale: Verbal asks get re-litigated at invoice; written approval is the legal anchor.

rule.statement: End of each week: send hours-spent summary by task. Surprises minimized when summary is weekly, not monthly.
rule.rationale: Monthly summaries surprise; weekly creates rhythm + no-surprise norm.

rule.statement: If client wants overage, options: (a) bump hourly rate temporarily, (b) defer to next week, (c) hire more freelancers. Choice in client's hands.
rule.rationale: Without explicit overage policy, freelancers absorb overage as unbilled work; policy preserves margin.

rule.statement: If all Applies-If preconditions hold and no Skip-If disqualifier is present, execute the full procedure from `04-procedure.xml` and commit the artefact described in `02-output-contract.xml`.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's affirmative leaf; pairs the preconditions with the methodology's commit path.

rule.statement: If any Skip-If condition is present (one-shot, <3 instances/year, regulator override, missing owner), do NOT apply this methodology. Route to the sibling/upstream methodology named in `AGENTS.md` Related section.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's skip leaf; prevents over-application of a heavyweight artefact when the loop conditions do not hold.
